name: TanStack Router
description: Type-safe-first router with typed search params and built-in data loading; basis for TanStack Start

name.comment: Official name; primary React package is @tanstack/react-router.

description.value: A client-first, server-capable, fully type-safe router for React with schema-validated search params and built-in data loading

category.comment: Client-first drop-in router you add to an existing React app; the full-stack meta-framework TanStack Start is a separate candidate built on top of it.

initial-release.comment: First public releases (v0 beta) landed in 2022 as the successor to React Location; stable v1.0 reached December 2023.

npm-weekly-downloads.comment: Weekly downloads of @tanstack/react-router for the week of 2026-07-07 to 2026-07-13.

routing-paradigm.comment: Supports both code-based routes (createRoute config objects) and file-based routing via the Vite plugin / CLI; both can be mixed.

optional-catch-all-params.comment: Supports splat/wildcard and optional path segments.

search-params-api.comment: First-class, schema-validated typed search params — a headline feature; params are parsed/serialized as JSON and validated.

route-guards-middleware.comment: beforeLoad hook acts as a per-route guard/middleware in the load lifecycle.

written-in-typescript.comment: Authored in TypeScript with first-class type definitions; type safety is its primary design goal.

type-safe-paths-links.comment: Link/navigate targets are checked against the known route tree.

type-safe-search-params.comment: Search params are typed and validated (typically via a schema/validator).

codegen-required.comment: Type safety is inference-based, not codegen-based. File-based routing optionally generates a routeTree.gen.ts via the Vite plugin/CLI for convenience, but code-based routing needs no generation step.

type-safety-rating.comment: End-to-end inferred type safety across paths, params, search params, loaders, and context — the strongest in this comparison (guideline level 5).

route-data-loaders.comment: Built-in route loaders with SWR caching and useLoaderData.

actions-mutations.comment: No built-in route action/mutation primitive; the docs direct mutations to external libraries such as TanStack Query, then invalidate router state. (Server functions/actions live in TanStack Start.)

pending-transition-ui.comment: Built-in pending components and React transition integration during navigation.

scroll-restoration.comment: Built-in scroll restoration including hash and top-of-page scrolling.

per-route-code-splitting.comment: Async route elements / lazy route components enable per-route code splitting.

ssr-support.comment: SSR is documented (renderRouterToString, RouterServer/RouterClient) but requires manual server/client entry setup and is marked experimental until TanStack Start is stable; turnkey SSR is provided by TanStack Start.

react-server-components.comment: The standalone router does not offer first-class RSC; RSC work is being developed in TanStack Start rather than the router core.

static-site-generation.comment: The standalone router does not itself pre-render routes to static HTML; prerendering/SSG is a TanStack Start capability.

client-only-usable.comment: Client-first: runs as a pure CSR SPA router with no server required.

framework-bound.comment: Drop-in router for any React app; does not require its own toolchain (that is TanStack Start).

bundle-size.comment: @tanstack/react-router core, minified + gzipped (~39.6 KB), per Bundlephobia (checked 2026-07-14).

zero-runtime-dependencies.comment: Effectively no third-party runtime deps; its ~4 runtime dependencies are all first-party TanStack packages (router-core, history, store).

tree-shakeable.comment: Ships ESM and supports tree-shaking / dead-code elimination.

documentation-quality.comment: Comprehensive docs: guides, API reference, runnable examples, migration paths, and comparison tables (guideline level 5).

official-devtools.comment: Dedicated TanStack Router Devtools (separate @tanstack/router-devtools package) for inspecting routes and navigation.

data-fetching-integration.comment: Built-in SWR-cached loaders plus documented integration with external data libraries including TanStack Query, SWR, and Apollo.

predecessor-lineage.comment: TanStack Router evolved from the React Location project; TanStack Start is the full-stack framework built on top of it.
